text:
Athletics, theParis 2024 Games'main attraction, has finally kicked off - much to the joy of fans worldwide. Following the thrilling aquatics and gymnastics events in the French capital, athletics began with its first contest - the men's 20km Race Walk - on Thursday morning.

The full schedule commenced on Friday, featuring theDecathlon 100m, Hammer Throw qualification, Women's High Jump qualification, and Women's 100m Preliminary Rounds, among others.Track and fieldevents are now in full swing, with spectators eagerly watching favourites such as thehurdles,men's and women's 100 metre raceand4x100m relay.

The grand finale will be theWomen's Marathonon August 11. Providing expert commentary across the 48 events is theBBC'stop-notch team of commentators, including:

Steve Cram, a dominant middle-distance runner from the 1980s, will lend his expertise to theBBC'sathletics coverage. Known as 'The Jarrow Arrow', Cram set world records in the 1500m, 2000m and the mile within 19 days in the summer of 1985.

As the first man to run 1500m under 3 minutes and 30 seconds, the silver medallist became theBBC'slead commentator for athletics events at the London 2012 Olympics. He has since been a regular feature at other Olympic Games and their winter editions.

Andrew Cotter, a familiar voice to Paris Olympics viewers, was lauded for his impromptu translations of various speeches during the opening ceremony last week. Known for his golf and rugby coverage, Cotter has been part ofBBCSport's athletics commentary team since 2013.



The Scottish commentator, who played golf for Scottish Schools and Scottish Universities, started his broadcasting career at an Edinburgh radio station before joining BBC Radio 5 Live and Sky News. The 51-year-old multi-sport commentator has been the voice of Olympics athletics, including its opening and closing ceremonies, since 2016.

Steve Backley brings two decades of top-level athletics experience to his expert analysis of field events. He once held the javelin world record with a 91.46m throw in 1992 and won two Olympic silver medals and a bronze. Backley succeeded Paul Dickenson as BBC Sport's lead field commentator, reaching millions at every World, European and Olympic Games since Beijing. His most memorable moments include calling Jessica Ennis and Greg Rutherford's victories.

Colin Jackson is a beloved national figure and one of Britain's most famous athletes due to his success in the 110m hurdles, where he won silver at the Seoul 1988 Games. His world record of 12.91 seconds for the 110m hurdles remained unbroken for nearly 13 years.

The Welshman, who first lent his voice to the 2004 Olympics, has been a key member of theBBC'sathletics commentary team at every Games since. He has also graced our screens on shows likeStrictly Come Dancing,Who Do You Think You Are? and Dancing on Ice.



Competing in four consecutive Olympic Games from 1996 to 2008, Paula Radcliffe clinched gold in the marathon at the 2005 World Championships in Helsinki. She also triumphed at both the London and New York marathons three times and held the fastest women's marathon time for an impressive 16 years.

Earlier this week, the 50-year-old Cheshire native found herself in a controversy after wishing convicted rapist Steven van de Velde "best of luck" ahead of his beach volleyball matches. Her comments, aired on LBC radio, led to her issuing an apology, expressing that she was "very sorry" and "mortified".

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

For the past decade, Jazmin Sawyers has been one of Britain's top long jumpers, competing in the last two Olympic Games. The multi-talented athlete began her sporting journey as a gymnast at the tender age of four, transitioning to athletics at 10 when she joined the City of Stoke Athletics Club.

She secured a bronze medal at the 2022 European Championships and clinched gold in the same event at the 2023 European Indoor Championships. However, her aspirations of winning an Olympic gold were dashed due to an Achilles rupture earlier this year, causing her to miss the Paris games. She will now lend her expertise as a commentator.

title: Full list of BBC athletics commentators for 2024 Olympics | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Kasra Moradi, Aaron Morris
description: The Olympic Games are well underway in Paris, and a familiar team of BBC commentators will be providing expert analysis across countless events.
